@@ -397,7 +397,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-2qmr4","title":"Proof: Cancellation Terminates Under Budget","description":"Prove bounded termination of cancellation protocol using a Lyapunov/potential argument (Σ obligations + pending finalizers + remaining budget).
Show that under sufficient budgets, cancel→drain→finalize terminates and cannot livelock.
Acceptance: proof + bound statement that is checkable in tests (upper bound on steps/polls).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"MaroonElk","created_at":"2026-02-03T18:01:41.221802010Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T05:28:26.957798877Z","closed_at":"2026-02-04T05:28:26.957724098Z","close_reason":"All acceptance criteria met: Lyapunov cancel_potential function, constructive cancel_protocol_terminates theorem (mask+3 steps by induction), cancel_steps_testable_bound (max 67 steps with MAX_MASK_DEPTH=64), plus termination from all mid-protocol states.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2qmr4","depends_on_id":"bd-2497b","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T18:01:41.229359130Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2qmr4","depends_on_id":"bd-330st","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T18:08:28.511636471Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2qmr4","depends_on_id":"bd-hf5bz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T18:08:16.716526462Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2qqyi","title":"Cancellation State Machine Spec + Validator","description":"Specify the cancellation state machine with explicit transitions, invariants, and witness validation rules.
Add a runtime validator that rejects out-of-order transitions in debug/lab mode.
Acceptance: spec doc + state transition table + tests for invalid transitions.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T18:02:57.695660621Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T05:31:14.219668955Z","closed_at":"2026-02-04T05:31:14.219569841Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2qqyi","depends_on_id":"bd-330st","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T18:09:28.900594251Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2qqyi","depends_on_id":"bd-38o3r","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T18:02:57.704651668Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2qwej","title":"Perf: baseline+profile scheduler benchmark (Round 1)","description":"Establish baseline (hyperfine) + CPU profile (flamegraph) for scheduler_benchmark; build opportunity matrix and implement one high-score optimization with isomorphism proof and golden outputs.","notes":"Perf profiling via perf record on cargo bench (after CARGO_PROFILE_BENCH_DEBUG build) for schedule_ready_pop: hotspots include RawTable::remove_entry (~15%), Scheduler::schedule (~16%), Scheduler::pop (~10%), HashMap::insert (~10%), BinaryHeap::pop (~2%). Baseline schedule_ready_pop: ~107.34 ns median ([104.66..110.46] ns). Current run: ~65.38 ns median ([64.94..66.19] ns), ~41% faster. Note: perf capture on bench binary directly ran in quick mode; profiling required perf record around cargo bench to get samples (~3k).","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"AmberCastle","created_at":"2026-02-05T08:04:06.319481236Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T00:51:14.736142009Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2qwej","depends_on_id":"bd-3orlh","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T08:04:06.356880781Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":213,"issue_id":"bd-2qwej","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Perf round update: ran scheduler_benchmark baseline + perf record (schedule_ready_pop, 0.5s measurement). Data: /tmp/perf_cargo_bench_ready_v4.data. schedule_ready_pop ~[59.96..65.10]ns (no change), batch_schedule_then_pop/10 ~[890..912]ns (noise). Tried inline ScheduledSet (small-inline + spill) to reduce HashSet ops; it regressed dedup_same_task and schedule_ready_pop, so reverted. Fixed build error in scheduler: ScheduledSet::with_capacity now uses DetHashSet::with_hasher(DetBuildHasher) + reserve. Next candidate hotspot per perf: memmove/allocs around BinaryHeap growth + schedule/pop.","created_at":"2026-02-05T20:08:54Z"},{"id":246,"issue_id":"bd-2qwej","author":"CobaltBadger","text":"I prototyped a high-impact Scheduler dedup optimization (dense ScheduledSet with overflow HashSet) that improved criterion bench scheduler/throughput/schedule_pop_cycle/10000 from ~1.606ms median to ~1.034ms median (about 37% faster; throughput +59%). I cannot land it yet because src/runtime/scheduler/priority.rs currently has an exclusive AgentMail reservation by ChartreuseCove; I sent them a coordination message offering the patch.","created_at":"2026-02-06T00:37:12Z"},{"id":248,"issue_id":"bd-2qwej","author":"CobaltBadger","text":"Landed perf patch in commit 7ba0016: Scheduler ScheduledSet now uses dense generation tokens (Vec<u64>) with overflow DetHashSet fallback; reduces HashSet traffic on schedule/pop. Measured on criterion scheduler/throughput/schedule_pop_cycle/10000: baseline ~1.606ms median -> ~1.036ms median (~37% faster; throughput +59%). Quality gates: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy -D warnings, cargo test all pass.","created_at":"2026-02-06T00:51:14Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-2qx15","title":"[SPORK][Obs] Conformal Calibration for Health Thresholds","description":"Where Spork needs thresholds (queue depth, restart latency), integrate conformal calibration so false alarms remain controlled under drift.","acceptance_criteria":"Acceptance:

- Coverage diagnostics are reported deterministically in lab.","status":"in_progress","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"MagentaMarsh","created_at":"2026-02-06T03:20:51.052613466Z","created_by":"FuchsiaRiver","updated_at":"2026-02-06T11:29:43.834053025Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformal","observability","spork"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2qx15","depends_on_id":"bd-iu07v","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T03:20:51.127319544Z","created_by":"FuchsiaRiver"}]}

 {"id":"bd-2qyj","title":"[AA] Cancellation Protocol + Obligation Ledger","description":"Define and prove the cancellation protocol as a multi‑phase state machine with budgets; enforce linear obligations (permits/acks/leases) and loser‑drain semantics.

Why: cancellation is a protocol, not a flag. This epic ensures no obligation leaks and bounded cleanup.

Done when: protocol is formalized, implemented, and enforced across combinators + runtime.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-03T00:04:22.489018057Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T10:15:55.775354022Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T10:15:55.775280014Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2qyj","depends_on_id":"bd-we5i","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T00:04:22.496969943Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2qym","title":"Ecosystem adapters + interop boundaries","description":"Goal: adapters for popular Rust ecosystem patterns (tower-like Service, hyper-style request/response types, serde integration) that preserve Asupersync semantics. No compatibility shims or deprecated API wrappers; use explicit boundary types and conversion layers with clear documentation. Include unit tests for cancellation/obligation invariants at adapter boundaries.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:51:49.084482123Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T05:58:33.064023915Z","closed_at":"2026-02-02T05:58:33.063957602Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["adapters","ecosystem"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2qym","depends_on_id":"bd-30c5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T00:02:10Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2qym","depends_on_id":"bd-qe1u","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T00:02:10Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":100,"issue_id":"bd-2qym","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Code-complete: Tower<->Asupersync bidirectional adapters (TowerAdapter, TowerAdapterWithProvider, AsupersyncAdapter), CxProvider trait, CancellationMode, ErrorAdapter, MapResponse/MapErr. Migration framework in src/migration/ with DualValue and MigrationMode. 22 tests across service and migration modules.","created_at":"2026-02-02T05:58:30Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2r4fy","title":"[SPORK][AI] Trace/Event Taxonomy: stable names, structured fields, grepability","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T05:46:07.318454581Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T05:46:56.315252273Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ai","observability","spork","trace"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2r4fy","depends_on_id":"bd-11dm5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T06:36:12Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2r4fy","depends_on_id":"bd-11z9a","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T06:36:12Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2r4fy","depends_on_id":"bd-35itx","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T06:36:12Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":273,"issue_id":"bd-2r4fy","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Goal

Stabilize the trace/event vocabulary so debugging can be automated.

Deliverables:
- naming conventions for events
- stable field sets (no ad-hoc strings)
- grepability: agents can search for event kinds and map to docs

## Acceptance

- Event taxonomy is documented and tested.
- Any new trace event kind requires a doc entry.
","created_at":"2026-02-06T05:46:56Z"}]}
